---
title: "Analysis"
author: "Jim Denne"
date: "2026-05-17"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

# Setup

```{r Setup, echo = TRUE, results = 'asis'}
library(knitr)

set.seed(1)
source("src/data_calibration.R")
source("src/severity_models.R")
source("src/reinsurance.R")
source("src/confidence_intervals.R")
source("src/ruin_simulation.R")

# Load and calibrate data

raw <- load_fremtpl2(
  freq_path = "data/freMTPL2freq.rda",
  sev_path  = "data/freMTPL2sev.rda"
)

freq <- extract_claim_frequency(raw$freq)
sev <- extract_claim_amounts(raw$sev)

emp_model <- empirical_severity_model(sev)

# Since lambda is annual, t is measured in years.
t_years <- 5

# Chosen business assumptions
portfolio_size <- 1000          # keeps simulation computationally manageable
capital_multiple <- 0.5         # c0 = 0.5x annual expected claims
theta <- 0.10                   # insurer premium loading
xi <- 0.15                      # reinsurer loading, assumed > insurer loading
ruin_limit <- 0.01              # 1% 5-year ruin threshold

lambda_portfolio <- portfolio_size * freq$lambda_per_policy
annual_expected_claims <- lambda_portfolio * emp_model$mean
c0 <- capital_multiple * annual_expected_claims

# Calibration summary ---------------------------------------------------------
calibration_summary <- data.frame(
  Quantity = c(
    "Total claims",
    "Total exposure",
    "Lambda per policy",
    "Portfolio size",
    "Lambda portfolio",
    "Severity mean",
    "Annual expected claims",
    "Initial capital",
    "Reinsurance loading theta",
    "Safety loading xi"
  ),
  Value = c(
    freq$total_claims,
    freq$total_exposure,
    freq$lambda_per_policy,
    portfolio_size,
    lambda_portfolio,
    emp_model$mean,
    annual_expected_claims,
    c0,
    theta,
    xi
  )
)

kable(
  calibration_summary,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Calibration inputs and fitted portfolio quantities"
)

# Severity summary ------------------------------------------------------------
severity_summary <- data.frame(
  Metric = c(
    "Number of claims",
    "Mean severity",
    "Median severity",
    "90th percentile",
    "95th percentile",
    "99th percentile",
    "Maximum severity"
  ),
  Value = c(
    length(sev),
    mean(sev),
    median(sev),
    as.numeric(quantile(sev, 0.90)),
    as.numeric(quantile(sev, 0.95)),
    as.numeric(quantile(sev, 0.99)),
    max(sev)
  )
)

kable(
  severity_summary,
  digits = 2,
  caption = "Empirical severity distribution summary"
)
```

# Ruin Simulation 

```{r Ruin Simulation, echo = TRUE, results = 'asis'}

set.seed(1)
# Baseline no-reinsurance run
n_baseline <- 50000

baseline <- evaluate_policy(
  n_sim = n_baseline,
  t = t_years,
  lambda = lambda_portfolio,
  sev_model = emp_model,
  policy = "none",
  c0 = c0,
  theta = theta,
  xi = 0
)

# Display results
baseline_table <- baseline

baseline_table$alpha <- ifelse(is.na(baseline_table$alpha), "-", baseline_table$alpha)
baseline_table$d     <- ifelse(is.na(baseline_table$d), "-", baseline_table$d)

kable(
  baseline_table,
  digits = 4,
  col.names = c(
    "Policy",
    "Alpha",
    "Deductible",
    "Ruin Probability",
    "CI Lower",
    "CI Upper",
    "Expected Profit"
  ),
  caption = "Baseline policy performance"
)

```

# Reinsurance Optimisation

```{r Helpers, echo = TRUE}
select_best_contract_ci <- function(results, ruin_limit = 0.01) {
  feasible <- results[results$ci_upper <= ruin_limit, ]

  if (nrow(feasible) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  feasible[which.max(feasible$expected_profit), ]
}

print_candidates <- function(...) {
  candidates <- Filter(Negate(is.null), list(...))
  if (length(candidates) == 0) return(NULL)
  do.call(rbind, candidates)
}

format_candidates <- function(x) {
  if (is.null(x) || nrow(x) == 0) {
    return(data.frame(Message = "No feasible contracts found"))
  }

  x$alpha <- ifelse(is.na(x$alpha), "-", x$alpha)
  x$d     <- ifelse(is.na(x$d), "-", x$d)

  x
}

```

## Rough Grid Search

```{r Rough Grid Search, echo = TRUE, results = 'asis'}
set.seed(1)

n_grid_rough <- 10000

# Net profit condition for proportional
# alpha > 1 - theta / xi
alpha_min_npc <- max(0, 1 - theta / xi)

alpha_grid_rough <- seq(
  from = alpha_min_npc + 0.01,
  to = 1.00,
  by = 0.025
)

# Rough EoL grid:
# Mix empirical quantiles with evenly spaced values.
d_grid_rough <- sort(unique(c(
  as.numeric(quantile(sev, probs = seq(0.50, 0.995, length.out = 35), type = 8)),
  seq(1000, 20000, by = 1000)
)))

# Rough proportional grid search ----------------------------------------------
prop_results_rough <- grid_search_proportional(
  alpha_grid = alpha_grid_rough,
  n_sim = n_grid_rough,
  t = t_years,
  lambda = lambda_portfolio,
  sev_model = emp_model,
  c0 = c0,
  theta = theta,
  xi = xi
)

# Rough EoL grid search -------------------------------------------------------
eol_results_rough <- grid_search_eol(
  d_grid = d_grid_rough,
  n_sim = n_grid_rough,
  t = t_years,
  lambda = lambda_portfolio,
  sev_model = emp_model,
  c0 = c0,
  theta = theta,
  xi = xi
)

best_prop_point_rough <- select_best_contract(prop_results_rough,
                                              ruin_limit = ruin_limit)
best_eol_point_rough <- select_best_contract(eol_results_rough, 
                                             ruin_limit = ruin_limit)

best_prop_ci_rough <- select_best_contract_ci(prop_results_rough, 
                                              ruin_limit = ruin_limit)
best_eol_ci_rough <- select_best_contract_ci(eol_results_rough, 
                                             ruin_limit = ruin_limit)

# Display results -------------------------------------------------------------
rough_point_candidates <- format_candidates(print_candidates(
  best_prop_point_rough,
  best_eol_point_rough
))

rough_ci_candidates <- format_candidates(print_candidates(
  best_prop_ci_rough,
  best_eol_ci_rough
))

kable(
  rough_point_candidates,
  digits = 4,
  col.names = c(
    "Policy",
    "Alpha",
    "Deductible",
    "Ruin Probability",
    "CI Lower",
    "CI Upper",
    "Expected Profit"
  ),
  caption = "Rough best contracts under point estimate constraint"
)

kable(
  rough_ci_candidates,
  digits = 4,
  col.names = c(
    "Policy",
    "Alpha",
    "Deductible",
    "Ruin Probability",
    "CI Lower",
    "CI Upper",
    "Expected Profit"
  ),
  caption = "Rough best contracts under Wilson upper bound constraint"
)

```

## Fine Grid Search

```{r Fine Grid Search}

set.seed(1)

n_grid_fine <- 50000

# Proportional fine grid ------------------------------------------------------
# If rough search found a feasible proportional contract, refine near it.
if (!is.null(best_prop_point_rough)) {
  alpha_centre <- best_prop_point_rough$alpha

  alpha_grid_fine <- seq(
    from = max(alpha_min_npc + 0.001, alpha_centre - 0.075),
    to = min(1.00, alpha_centre + 0.075),
    by = 0.005
  )
  
  prop_results_fine <- grid_search_proportional(
    alpha_grid = alpha_grid_fine,
    n_sim = n_grid_fine,
    t = t_years,
    lambda = lambda_portfolio,
    sev_model = emp_model,
    c0 = c0,
    theta = theta,
    xi = xi
  )

} else {
  cat("\nNo feasible proportional contract found in rough search.\n")
  prop_results_fine <- prop_results_rough
}


# EoL fine grid ---------------------------------------------------------------
# Prefer conservative rough candidate if available, otherwise use point estimate.
eol_centre <- if (!is.null(best_eol_ci_rough)) {
  best_eol_ci_rough$d
} else if (!is.null(best_eol_point_rough)) {
  best_eol_point_rough$d
} else {
  median(d_grid_rough)
}

d_grid_fine <- seq(
  from = max(100, eol_centre - 3000),
  to = eol_centre + 3000,
  by = 250
)

eol_results_fine <- grid_search_eol(
  d_grid = d_grid_fine,
  n_sim = n_grid_fine,
  t = t_years,
  lambda = lambda_portfolio,
  sev_model = emp_model,
  c0 = c0,
  theta = theta,
  xi = xi
)

grid_results <- rbind(prop_results_fine, eol_results_fine)

best_prop_point <- select_best_contract(
  prop_results_fine,
  ruin_limit = ruin_limit
)

best_eol_point <- select_best_contract(
  eol_results_fine,
  ruin_limit = ruin_limit
)

best_prop_conservative <- select_best_contract_ci(
  prop_results_fine,
  ruin_limit = ruin_limit
)

best_eol_conservative <- select_best_contract_ci(
  eol_results_fine,
  ruin_limit = ruin_limit
)

# Display results -------------------------------------------------------------
fine_point_candidates <- format_candidates(print_candidates(
  best_prop_point,
  best_eol_point
))

fine_ci_candidates <- format_candidates(print_candidates(
  best_prop_conservative,
  best_eol_conservative
))

kable(
  fine_point_candidates,
  digits = 4,
  col.names = c(
    "Policy",
    "Alpha",
    "Deductible",
    "Ruin Probability",
    "CI Lower",
    "CI Upper",
    "Expected Profit"
  ),
  caption = "Fine best contracts under point estimate constraint"
)

kable(
  fine_ci_candidates,
  digits = 4,
  col.names = c(
    "Policy",
    "Alpha",
    "Deductible",
    "Ruin Probability",
    "CI Lower",
    "CI Upper",
    "Expected Profit"
  ),
  caption = "Fine best contracts under Wilson upper bound constraint"
)

```

# Final Values 

```{r Final Values}

prop_candidate <- if (!is.null(best_prop_conservative)) {
  best_prop_conservative
} else {
  best_prop_point
}

eol_candidate <- if (!is.null(best_eol_conservative)) {
  best_eol_conservative
} else {
  best_eol_point
}

evaluate_candidate <- function(candidate, n_final = 200000) {
  if (is.null(candidate)) return(NULL)

  evaluate_policy(
    n_sim = n_final,
    t = t_years,
    lambda = lambda_portfolio,
    sev_model = emp_model,
    policy = candidate$policy,
    c0 = c0,
    theta = theta,
    xi = xi,
    d = if (is.na(candidate$d)) NULL else candidate$d,
    alpha = if (is.na(candidate$alpha)) NULL else candidate$alpha
  )
}

# Final Results ---------------------------------------------------------------
set.seed(1)
n_final <- 200000

baseline_final <- evaluate_policy(
  n_sim = n_final,
  t = t_years,
  lambda = lambda_portfolio,
  sev_model = emp_model,
  policy = "none",
  c0 = c0,
  theta = theta,
  xi = 0
)

prop_final <- evaluate_candidate(prop_candidate, n_final = n_final)
eol_final <- evaluate_candidate(eol_candidate, n_final = n_final)

final_results <- do.call(rbind, Filter(Negate(is.null), list(
  baseline_final,
  prop_final,
  eol_final
)))

format_final_results <- function(x) {
  x$alpha <- ifelse(is.na(x$alpha), "-", x$alpha)
  x$d     <- ifelse(is.na(x$d), "-", x$d)
  x
}

final_comparison_table <- format_final_results(final_results)

kable(
  final_comparison_table,
  digits = 4,
  col.names = c(
    "Policy",
    "Alpha",
    "Deductible",
    "Ruin Probability",
    "CI Lower",
    "CI Upper",
    "Expected Profit"
  ),
  caption = "Final reinsurance comparison"
)

# Capital efficiency summary --------------------------------------------------
baseline_profit <- baseline_final$expected_profit
baseline_ruin   <- baseline_final$ruin_prob

final_results$profit_sacrifice <- baseline_profit - final_results$expected_profit
final_results$ruin_reduction   <- baseline_ruin - final_results$ruin_prob

# Ruin cost := profit reduction per 1 percentage decrease in ruin reduction
final_results$ruin_cost <- ifelse(
  final_results$profit_sacrifice > 0,
  final_results$profit_sacrifice / (100 * final_results$ruin_reduction),
  NA
)

final_efficiency_table <- format_final_results(final_results)

kable(
  final_efficiency_table,
  digits = 8,
  col.names = c(
    "Policy",
    "Alpha",
    "Deductible",
    "Ruin Probability",
    "CI Lower",
    "CI Upper",
    "Expected Profit",
    "Profit Sacrifice",
    "Ruin Reduction",
    "Ruin Cost"
  ),
  caption = "Final results with capital efficiency"
)
```

# Plots

```{r Plots, echo = TRUE}
# Profit vs ruin probability --------------------------------------------------
plot(
  grid_results$ruin_prob,
  grid_results$expected_profit,
  pch = 19,
  xlab = "Estimated 5-year ruin probability",
  ylab = "Expected 5-year profit",
  main = "Profit vs Ruin Probability"
)
abline(v = ruin_limit, lty = 2)

legend(
  "topright",
  legend = c("1% ruin threshold"),
  lty = 2,
  bty = "n"
)

# Proportional: alpha vs ruin -------------------------------------------------
plot(
  prop_results_fine$alpha,
  prop_results_fine$ruin_prob,
  type = "b",
  pch = 19,
  xlab = "Retention rate alpha",
  ylab = "Estimated 5-year ruin probability",
  main = "Proportional Reinsurance: Ruin vs Retention"
)
abline(h = ruin_limit, lty = 2)

# EoL: deductible vs ruin  ----------------------------------------------------
plot(
  eol_results_fine$d,
  eol_results_fine$ruin_prob,
  type = "b",
  pch = 19,
  xlab = "Deductible d",
  ylab = "Estimated 5-year ruin probability",
  main = "EoL Reinsurance: Ruin vs Deductible"
)
abline(h = ruin_limit, lty = 2)

```
